message:
fix(abi): thread-safe fallback errno, lgamma/lgammaf signgam update, feupdateenv error propagation, and tgammaf(0) ERANGE

errno_abi: The static mut FALLBACK_ERRNO (used when TLS is unavailable during
thread teardown) was a data race — all threads shared one mutable word.
Replaces with a LazyLock<Mutex<HashMap<ThreadId, Box<c_int>>>> that gives
each thread its own stable fallback slot. Two new tests verify per-thread
stability and cross-thread isolation.

math_abi: lgamma/lgammaf were not updating the global signgam/__signgam
variables, which glibc users depend on to recover the sign of Gamma(x).
Now calls lgamma_r/lgammaf_r first to get the sign, writes it to both
globals, then proceeds with the existing unary_entry path. Also fixes
tgammaf(0) which was not setting ERANGE (pole error) and adds logb(0)
ERANGE (pole error: returns -Infinity).

fenv_abi: feupdateenv was ignoring the return value of fesetenv — if the
environment pointer was invalid (null), fesetenv returned -1 but
feupdateenv still proceeded to feraiseexcept and returned 0 (success).
Now propagates the error. New test feupdateenv_null_is_rejected verifies.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
